transcript:
What you're watching is scatter becoming orbit — hundreds of scattered fragments pulled into five spinning knots of light, each one a swirl of color that keeps circling, never landing.

Here's the trick: no single element can display the full palette. Look in the spectral identities near the top — each seeker only "carries" a partial slice, like onlyRed or redAndBlue. They're drawn toward the nearest void — that's literally what the code calls its attractors, "lacunae: gaps in understanding" — but they never arrive. In the draw loop, there's an orbital force pulling sideways as well as inward, which is why they spiral around the void instead of crashing into it. The white-hot rings you see are additive overlap doing real color math in real time — magenta and white showing up on screen even though no single seeker was ever assigned those colors.

The reframe: those glowing circles aren't destinations, they're the visible residue of never arriving — completion isn't in this piece at all, only the shape of wanting.
